citeinfo.title: Cyanotoxin, chlorophyll-a, and cyanobacterial toxin genetic data for samples collected at twelve large river sites throughout the United States, June through October 2019
citeinfo.geoform: Excel Spreadsheet and Comma Separated Values (CSV) file

descript.abstract: This U.S. Geological Survey (USGS) data release provides discretely measured cyanotoxin, chlorophyll-a, and cyanobacterial toxin genetic data for samples collected from twelve large river sites throughout the United States, from June through October 2019. Discrete water-quality samples were analyzed for cyanotoxins (anatoxin, cylindrospermopsin, microcystin, and saxitoxin), chlorophyll-a, and cyanobacterial toxin genetics as part of a National Water-Quality Assessment Project pilot study to describe cyanobacteria and cyanotoxin occurrence in the Nation's large rivers. The data release contains the genetic data (in .csv and .xlsx formats), qPCR standard curve information (in .csv and .xlsx formats), and a readme file explaining the data headers for the genetic and standard curve files.
descript.purpose: These data were collected by the National Water-Quality Assessment Project as part of a pilot study to describe cyanobacteria and cyanotoxin occurrence in the Nation's large rivers. This data release was produced in compliance with the federal open-data requirements as a way to make scientific products associated with USGS research efforts and publications available to the public.

spdom.descgeog: Data were collected from twelve sites throughout the United States: Connecticut River at Thompsonville, CT (USGS Station Identification Number 01184000); Hudson River near Poughkeepsie, NY (USGS Station Identification Number 01372043); Delaware River at Trenton, NJ (USGS Station Identification Number 01463500); Susquehanna River at Conowingo, MD (USGS Station Identification Number 01578310); Chattahoochee River near Whitesburg, GA (USGS Station Identification Number 02338000); Ohio River at Cannelton Dam at Cannelton, IN (USGS Station Identification Number 03303280); Mississippi River at Hastings, MN (USGS Station Identification Number 05331580); Kansas River at De Soto, KS (USGS Station Identification Number 06892350); Missouri River at Hermann, MO (USGS Station Identification Number 06934500); Trinity River Below Dallas, TX (USGS Station Identification Number 08057410); Sacramento River at Freeport, CA (USGS Station Identification Number 11447650); and Willamette River at Portland, OR (USGS Station Identification Number 14211720).

place.placekey: Ohio River at Cannelton Dam at Cannelton, IN
Kansas River at De Soto, KS

idinfo.useconst: Public domain data from the U.S. Government are freely redistributable with proper metadata and source attribution. Please recognize the U.S. Geological Survey as the source of this information. Users are advised to read the dataset's metadata thoroughly to understand appropriate data limitations.

cntperp.cntorg: U.S. Geological Survey, New York Water Science Center

idinfo.datacred: This study was conducted by the U.S. Geological Survey National Water-Quality Assessment Project.

attracc.attraccr: All data were broadly checked for accuracy; no obvious outliers were present in the data.

dataqual.logic: No formal logical accuracy tests were conducted, but data were checked for consistency and numerical errors.
dataqual.complete: Dataset is considered complete for the information presented, as described in the abstract. Users are advised to read the rest of the metadata record carefully for additional details.

horizpa.horizpar: No formal positional accuracy tests were conducted.

vertacc.vertaccr: No formal positional accuracy tests were conducted.

procstep.procdesc: All data were compiled into one spreadsheet for this data release.

method.methtype: Discrete water-quality sample collection and analysis.
method.methdesc: Discrete water-quality samples were collected from twelve large river sites in the United States, from June through October 2019, in accordance with U.S. Geological Survey protocols as described in Graham and others (2020) and Zuellig and others (2021). Samples were analyzed for total cyanotoxins (anatoxins, cylindrospermopsins, microcystins, and saxitoxins) by enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA), chlorophyll-a and the degradate pheophytin-a in accordance with U.S. Environmental Protection Agency method 445.0, and genes present in anatoxin (anaC), cylindrospermopsin (cyrA), Dolichospermum-, Microcystis-, and Planktothrix-specific microcystin (mcyE), and saxitoxin (stxA) synthetase gene clusters by quantitative polymerase chain reaction (qPCR). Details on the standard curve for the qPCR results are given in a table associated with the metadata and described as a separate entity below. Cyanotoxins were analyzed by the USGS Organic Geochemistry Research Laboratory (Lawrence, Kansas) as described in Loftin and others (2008) and Graham and others (2010). Chlorophyll-a and pheophytin-a were analyzed at the USGS National Water Quality Laboratory (Lakewood, Colorado), in accordance with the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency Method 445.0 as described in Arar and others (1997). Cyanobacterial genetics were analyzed at the USGS Ohio Microbiology Laboratory (Columbus, Ohio) as described in Stelzer and others (2013). Molecular assays for cyanobacterial genes were done to enumerate the following: mcyE genus-specific toxin genes for microcystin (Rantala and others, 2006 and Sipari and others, 2010), sxtA toxin gene for saxitoxin (Al-Tebrineh and others, 2012), anaC toxin gene for anatoxin-a (Sabart and others, 2015) and cyrA toxin gene for cylindrospermopsin (Al-Tebrineh and others, 2012). The limit of blank (LoB), limit of detection (LoD), and limit of quantification (LoQ) were calculated for each assay according to procedures in Francy and others (2017).

citeinfo.origin: Al-Tebrineh, J., Pearson, L.A., Yasar, S.A., and Neilan, B.A.
citeinfo.title: A multiplex qPCR targeting hepato- and neurotoxigenic cyanobacteria of global significance
U.S. Environmental Protection Agency Method 445.0, In vitro determination of chlorophyll a and pheophytin a in marine and freshwater algae by fluorescence

citeinfo.origin: Francy, D.S., Bushon, R.N., Cicale, J.R., Brady, A.M.G., Kephart, C.M., Stelzer, E.A., and Ecker, C.D.
citeinfo.title: Quality assurance/quality control manual: Ohio Water Microbiology Laboratory

citeinfo.origin: Graham, J.L., Dubrovsky, N.M., Foster, G.M., King, L.R., Loftin, K.A., Rosen, B.H., and Stelzer, E.A.
citeinfo.title: Cyanotoxin occurrence in large rivers of the United States

citeinfo.origin: Graham, J.L., Loftin, K.A., Meyer, M.T., and Ziegler, A.C.
citeinfo.title: Cyanotoxin mixtures and taste-and-odor compounds in cyanobacterial blooms from the midwestern United States

citeinfo.origin: Loftin, K.A., Meyer, M.T., Rubio, F., Kamp, L., Humphries, E., and Whereat, E.
citeinfo.title: Comparison of two cell lysis procedures for recovery of microcystins in water samples from Silver Lake in Dover, Delaware, with microcystin producing cyanobacterial accumulations

citeinfo.origin: Rantala, A., Rajaniemi-Wacklin,P., Lyra, C., Lepisto, L., Rintala, J., Mankiewicz-Boczek, J., and Sivonen, K.
citeinfo.title: Detection of microcystin-producing cyanobacteria in Finnish lakes with genus-specific microcystin synthetase gene E (mcyE) PCR and associations with environmental factors

citeinfo.origin: Sabart, M., Crenn, K., Perriere, F., Abila, A., Leremboure, M., Colombet, J., Jousse, C., and Latour, D.
citeinfo.title: Co-occurance of microcystin and anatoxin-a in the freshwater lake Aydat (France): analytical and molecular approaches during a three-year survey

citeinfo.origin: Sipari, H., Rantala-Ylinen, A., Jokela, J., Oksanen, I., and Sivonen, K.
citeinfo.title: Development of a chip assay and quantitative PCR for detecting microcystin synthetase E gene expression

citeinfo.origin: Stelzer, E.A., Loftin, K.A., and Struffolino, P.
citeinfo.title: Relations between DNA- and RNA-based molecular methods for cyanobacteria and microcystin concentration at Maumee Bay State Park Lakeside Beach, Oregon, Ohio, 2012

citeinfo.origin: Zuellig, R.E., Graham, J.L., Stelzer, E.A., Loftin, K.A., and Rosen, B.H.
citeinfo.title: Cyanobacteria, cyanotoxin synthetase gene, and cyanotoxin occurrence among selected large river sites of the conterminous United States, 2017-18

serinfo.issue: U.S. Geological Survey Scientific Investigation Report 2021-5121

spdoinfo.indspref: Connecticut River at Thompsonville, CT (01184000) Long= -72.60583 Lat= 41.98722; Hudson River near Poughkeepsie, NY (01372043) Long= -73.94111 Lat= 41.72167; Delaware River at Trenton, NJ (01463500) Long= -74.77806 Lat= 40.22167; Susquehanna River at Conowingo, MD (01578310) Long= -76.17444 Lat= 39.65789; Chattahoochee River near Whitesburg, GA (02338000) Long= -84.90083 Lat= 33.47694; Ohio River at Cannelton Dam at Cannelton, IN (03303280) Long= -86.70556 Lat= 37.89944; Mississippi River at Hastings, MN (05331580) Long= -92.84778 Lat= 44.74583; Kansas River at De Soto, KS (06892350) Long= -94.96444 Lat= 38.98333; Missouri River at Hermann, MO (06934500) Long= -91.43850 Lat= 38.70981; Trinity River below Dallas, TX (08057410) Long= -96.73556 Lat= 32.70750; Sacramento River at Freeport, CA (11447650) Long= -121.5003 Lat= 38.45611; Willamette River at Portland, OR (14211720) Long= -122.6692 Lat= 45.5175

enttyp.enttypd: Cyanotoxin, chlorophyll-a, and cyanobacterial toxin genetic data for samples collected at twelve large river sites throughout the United States, June through October 2019.

attr.attrdef: USGS station identification number where samples were collected.
USGS station name where samples were collected.

edom.edomvd: Connecticut River at Thompsonville, CT; Hudson River near Poughkeepsie, NY; Delaware River at Trenton, NJ; Susquehanna River at Conowingo, MD; Chattahoochee River near Whitesburg, GA; Ohio River at Cannelton Dam at Cannelton, IN; Mississippi River at Hastings, MN; Kansas River at De Soto, KS; Missouri River at Hermann, MO; Trinity River below Dallas, TX; Sacramento River at Freeport, CA; and Willamette River at Portland, OR

attr.attrdef: Date samples were collected.

edom.edomvd: Month/Day/Year

attr.attrdef: Time samples were collected.

edom.edomv: H:MM; "NA" indicates laboratory replicate and no time offset.
edom.edomvd: Hours:Minutes

attr.attrdef: Type of sample collected.

edom.edomvd: Primary environmental sample
Two or more samples of environmental water that are collected simultaneously or at approximately the same time in the field.
edom.edomvds: U.S. Geological Survey National Field Manual, Chapter A4

edom.edomvd: A sample collected in a single bottle that is split at the laboratory into two or more replicates after having been processed and preserved.
edom.edomvds: U.S. Geological Survey National Field Manual, Chapter A4

attr.attrlabl: Data qualifiers for Total microcystins plus nodularins
attr.attrdef: Data qualifiers for attribute labeled Total microcystins plus nodularins (ug/L)

edom.edomvd: Less than symbol indicates concentration was less than laboratory reporting level.
edom.edomvds: Kansas Water Science Center Organic Geochemistry Research Laboratory

attr.attrdef: Total microcystins plus nodularins, unfiltered water, freeze/thaw extraction, ADDA specific enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay, recoverable, micrograms per liter (USGS parameter code 89011); refer to prior attribute for associated data qualifier.
Data qualifiers for attribute labeled Total cylindrospermopsins (ug/L).

edom.edomvd: Less than symbol indicates concentration was less than laboratory reporting level.
edom.edomvds: Kansas Water Science Center Organic Geochemistry Research Laboratory

attr.attrdef: Total cylindrospermopsins, freeze/thaw extraction and then filtered (0.7 micron filter), enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay, recoverable, micrograms per liter (USGS parameter code 89005); refer to prior attribute for associated data qualifier; "NM" indicates value was not measured.
Data qualifiers for attribute labeled Total saxitoxins (ug/L).

edom.edomvd: Less than symbol indicates concentration was less than laboratory reporting level.
edom.edomvds: Kansas Water Science Center Organic Geochemistry Research Laboratory

attr.attrdef: Total saxitoxins, freeze/thaw extraction and then filtered (0.7 micron filter), enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay, recoverable, micrograms per liter (USGS parameter code 89003); refer to prior attribute for associated data qualifier; "NM" indicates value was not measured.
Data qualifiers for attribute labeled Anatoxin-a (method IMM24) (ug/L).

edom.edomvd: Less than symbol indicates concentration was less than laboratory reporting level.
edom.edomvds: Kansas Water Science Center Organic Geochemistry Research Laboratory

attr.attrdef: Anatoxin-a, water, unfiltered, recoverable, micrograms per liter (USGS parameter code 68287); refer to prior attribute for associated data qualifier; "NM" indicates value was not measured.
Chlorophyll a, phytoplankton, chromatographic-fluorometric method, micrograms per liter (USGS parameter code 70953); "NM" indicates value was not measured.
Pheophytin a, phytoplankton, micrograms per liter (USGS parameter code 62360); "NM" indicates value was not measured.

attr.attrdef: Data qualifiers for attribute labeled Anatoxin-a gene anaC (cp/100 mL).

edom.edomvd: Less than symbol indicates concentration was less than laboratory reporting level.
Indicates sample was above the detection limit but below the limit for accurate quantifications and is therefore estimated.

attr.attrdef: Anatoxin-a gene anaC, water, DNA based, recoverable, copies per 100 milliliters (cp/100 mL) (USGS parameter code 31795); refer to prior attribute for associated data qualifier; "NM" indicates value was not measured.

Indicates sample was above the detection limit but below the limit for accurate quantifications and is therefore estimated.

attr.attrdef: Saxitoxin gene sxtA, water, DNA based, recoverable, copies per 100 milliliters (cp/100 mL) (USGS parameter code 31797); refer to prior attribute for associated data qualifier; "NM" indicates value was not measured.

enttyp.enttypd: Standard curve information for quantitative polymerase chain reaction (qPCR) assays, 2019.

attr.attrdef: The assigned name for an assay. An assay is a PCR reaction mix that contains primers to amplify a specific target and a reagent to detect that amplified target.

edom.edomvd: Microcystis mcyE ( microcystin synthetase gene), Planktothrix mcyE, Dolichospermum mcyE, sxtA (saxitoxin synthetase gene), anaC (anatoxin-a synthetase gene), cyrA (cylindrospermopsin synthetase gene).

attr.attrdef: The log concentration range in copies per reaction volume of control material over which the standard curve is confirmed to be linear.

attrdomv.udom: The log concentration range of control material over which the standard curve is confirmed to be linear.

attr.attrdef: The calculation of efficiency of the PCR amplification. The amplification efficiency is calculated using the slope of the regression line in the standard curve. A slope close to -3.32 indicates optimal, 100% PCR amplification efficiency

attrdomv.udom: The range of efficiencies of PCR amplifications.

attr.attrdef: Range of regression coefficients calculated from the regression line in the standard curve. The R^2 value indicates the closeness of fit between the standard curve regression line and the individual Ct data points from the standard reactions. A value of 1.00 indicates a perfect fit between the regression line and the data points.

attrdomv.udom: Range of regression coefficients calculated from the regression line in the standard curve.

attr.attrdef: Limit of detection (LoD) is the lowest concentration that can be detected with 95% confidence. The LoD is determined by running a series of dilutions of the target with a minimum of 10 replicates per dilution. The dilutions used will vary depending on the sensitivity of the assay. The dilution with the lowest concentration of known target with detections greater than 95 percent is chosen as the LoD. The average Ct value for this dilution is used to calculate a concentration (copies/rxn) using the standard curve run with the dilution series.
Dilution factor used by laboratory for sample analysis.

cntaddr.address: Denver Federal Center, Building 810, Mail Stop 302

distinfo.distliab: Unless otherwise stated, all data, metadata and related materials are considered to satisfy the quality standards relative to the purpose for which the data were collected. Although these data and associated metadata have been reviewed for accuracy and completeness and approved for release by the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS), no warranty expressed or implied is made regarding the display or utility of the data on any other system or for general or scientific purposes, nor shall the act of distribution constitute any such warranty.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
